commentary_text: The model favors Australia at 44% while the market favors the draw at 42%, revealing a fundamental disagreement on match structure. The sharpest single gap appears on the Australia win, where the model stands 19 percentage points higher than the sportsbook consensus at 24%. The market's tilt toward a stalemate and a Paraguay upset may reflect recent lineup changes, injury developments, or current form trends that the model's Elo foundation cannot capture. This divergence surfaces a genuine tension between statistical ratings and real-money pricing; neither source has privileged access to ground truth.
